claim: Auditory entrainment is strengthened when people see congruent visual and auditory information.

result.claim: Auditory entrainment is strengthened when people see congruent visual and auditory information.
result.reasoning:
The claim that auditory entrainment is strengthened when people see congruent visual and auditory information is strongly supported by the evidence. Paper ID 14437255, "Congruent Visual Speech Enhances Cortical Entrainment to Continuous Auditory Speech in Noise-Free Conditions," directly states that "Congruent audiovisual speech enhances our ability to comprehend a speaker, even in noise-free conditions" (prop_2357) and that "The cortical representation of the speech envelope is enhanced by the presentation of congruent audiovisual speech in noise-free conditions" (prop_2360). It further specifies that this enhancement is "most prominent at the temporal scale corresponding to syllabic rate (2-6 Hz)" (prop_2361) and that "neural entrainment to the speech envelope is inhibited when the auditory and visual streams are incongruent both temporally and contextually" (prop_2362).

Additionally, paper ID 6955746, "Auditory Cortex Tracks Both Auditory and Visual Stimulus Dynamics Using Low-Frequency Neuronal Phase Modulation," supports this by indicating that "Low-frequency neuronal information lies at the basis of the synergistic coordination of information across auditory and visual streams" (prop_740) and that "Delta-theta phase modulation across early sensory areas plays a role in continuously tracking naturalistic audio-visual streams" (prop_743). These findings collectively demonstrate that congruent visual and auditory information positively influences auditory entrainment.

evidence.text: Neural entrainment to the speech envelope is inhibited when the auditory and visual streams are incongruent both temporally and contextually.
evidence.source: Congruent Visual Speech Enhances Cortical Entrainment to Continuous Auditory Speech in Noise-Free Conditions.
